description: This figure presents the isothermal phase diagram of the Fe-20Mn-Al-C quaternary system at 1100°C, with mass % Al (0 to 14) on the horizontal axis and mass % C (0 to approximately 2.5) on the vertical axis for a fixed 20 mass % Mn content. The diagram delineates four principal phase fields: a large central gamma (fcc austenite) single-phase region, a gamma + kappa two-phase region at higher Al and C contents, a gamma + alpha two-phase region at high Al and low C contents, and an M3C + gamma region at low Al and high C contents. Superimposed dashed contour lines represent estimated iso-density values of the fcc gamma phase, labeled 6.4, 6.6, 6.8, 7.0, 7.2, 7.4, 7.6, and 7.8 in g/cm³. The figure demonstrates that increasing Al strongly reduces alloy density, while the phase boundaries define the compositional window for single-phase gamma austenite with density below 7.0 g/cm³. This diagram provides the thermodynamic basis for designing the low-density, high-specific-strength Fe-Mn-Al-C(-Cr) alloys studied in the paper, linking composition, phase stability, and density.
